name: Modular flavor symmetry + string axiverse + KKLT moduli + type-I seesaw
summary: Classification: POSSIBLY NOVEL COMPOSITION. Surfaced by the catalog's never-composed-but-compatible pair analysis: modular_flavor_symmetry and string_axiverse share both `string_modulus` field content and the puzzle `flavor_mass_hierarchy`, but no canonical framework name composes them. The idea is to use the modulus tau of a finite modular group (level N <= 5 covers existing flavor models) as both the source of Yukawa structure (via modular forms) AND as one of the axiverse fields after compactification, with KKLT-style stabilisation pinning tau near a CP-conserving fixed point. The shared tau is the cross-bridge: it explains lepton mixing (modular flavor) and contributes to the axion pool (axiverse) simultaneously. Type-I seesaw provides the neutrino mass scale; CP violation comes from tau being off the fixed point at the level demanded by leptogenesis data. The composition is not (to our knowledge) a named program in the literature, though papers by Feruglio, Kobayashi, and collaborators since 2017 have explored individual edges of this graph. Listed here as the catalog's own discovery output — the framework that the search loop surfaces but no review article we know of has assembled.

lineage.citation: Feruglio, 'Are neutrino masses modular forms?', in 'From My Vast Repertoire... Guido Altarelli's Legacy' (2019)
Kobayashi, Tanaka, Tatsuishi, 'Neutrino mixing from finite modular groups', PRD 98, 016004 (2018)
Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell, 'String Axiverse', PRD 81, 123530 (2010)
Kachru, Kallosh, Linde, Trivedi, 'de Sitter vacua in string theory', PRD 68, 046005 (2003)
Ding, King, 'Neutrino mass and mixing with modular symmetry', Rept. Prog. Phys. 87, 084201 (2024)
